## Fases de Implementação

### Fase 0: Pré-requisitos
- Confirmar disponibilidade de VPC endpoints
- Criar chave KMS com configuração multi-região
- Criar secret no Secrets Manager para chave HMAC
- Configurar CloudWatch Logs Data Protection Policy
- Criar tabela DynamoDB do Circuit Breaker
- **Modificar FeeExemption Lambda para callback de sucesso**

### Fase 1: Componentes Core
- Deploy da Lambda DLQ-Consumer
- Criar DynamoDB Armazém de Falhas com GSI (incluindo novos campos)
- Deploy da Lambda Replay com controle de concorrência
- Configurar agendamento EventBridge

### Fase 2: Confiabilidade & Operações
- Deploy da Lambda de Recuperação de Travados
- Configurar EventBridge Pipes (substituindo Streams diretos)
- Deploy do TTL Archiver com verificação de archive_status
- Deploy da Lambda S3 Backup com replicação cross-region
- Configurar alarmes e dashboard CloudWatch

### Fase 3: API & LGPD
- Deploy da API Gateway Privada
- Implementar endpoints de direitos do titular (DELETE, EXPORT)
- Deploy da Lambda Health Check
- Habilitar tracing X-Ray
- Criar runbooks operacionais

### Fase 4: Validação
- Testes de integração com CPFs de teste
- Testes de caos (experimentos FIS)
- Exercícios de runbook com Squad Fênix
- Deploy canário (1% do tráfego, 48h de observação)

---

## Runbooks Operacionais (Resumo)

| ID | Runbook | Gatilho |
|----|---------|---------|
| RB-001 | Replay Manual por CPF | Solicitação de suporte para CPF específico |
| RB-002 | Replay em Massa Após Correção do CBILL | Issue do CBILL resolvido |
| RB-003 | Investigação de Circuit Breaker | Alarme CB OPEN |
| RB-004 | Recuperação de Registros Travados | Alarme de travados |
| RB-005 | Investigação de Falha do DLQ-Consumer | Alarme de erro do Consumer |
| RB-006 | Recuperação de Falha do TTL Archiver | Alarme da DLQ do Archiver |
| RB-007 | Escalamento de Capacidade do DynamoDB | Alarme de throttle |
| RB-008 | Rotação de Chave HMAC | Anual ou comprometimento |
| RB-009 | Override Manual do Circuit Breaker | Replay emergencial necessário |
| RB-010 | Investigação de Falha do Health Check | Endpoint health retorna unhealthy |
| RB-011 | Restauração de DR a partir do Backup S3 | Falha da região primária |
| RB-012 | Resolução de ConditionalCheckFailed | Alarme de concorrência |
| RB-013 | **NOVO**: Processamento de Direito do Titular LGPD | Solicitação de exclusão/exportação |
| RB-014 | **NOVO**: Registros Próximos ao TTL Sem Arquivo | Alarme de perda iminente |

---

## Log de Decisões

| Decisão | Justificativa | Alternativas Rejeitadas |
|---------|---------------|------------------------|
| DynamoDB ao invés de RDS | Serverless, pay-per-use, padrão KV simples | RDS (super-provisionado), Redis (sem durabilidade) |
| HMAC-SHA256 ao invés de hash simples | Proteção contra rainbow tables, chave secreta | SHA-256 puro (vulnerável), bcrypt (mais lento) |
| Secrets Manager ao invés de SSM | Rotação automática, auditoria nativa | SSM (sem rotação automática) |
| Versionamento de chave HMAC | Permite rotação sem perda de dados | Chave única (rotação = registros órfãos) |
| Circuit breaker em DynamoDB | Operações atômicas, persistente | SSM (condições de corrida), in-memory (stateless) |
| Classificação de erros | Evita false positives no circuit breaker | Tratamento uniforme (bloqueia desnecessariamente) |
| EventBridge Pipes | Leitura única do stream, filtragem antes de Lambda | Streams diretos (3x custo, acoplamento) |
| Backup S3 ao invés de Global Tables | Mais simples, mais barato, RPO adequado para SLA de 48h | Global Tables (2x custo, complexidade desnecessária) |
| API privada ao invés de pública | Uso apenas interno, segurança | Pública + WAF (custo desnecessário) |
| Controle de concorrência com lock | Previne thundering herd | Sem lock (registros processados múltiplas vezes) |
| Endpoints LGPD separados | Clareza, auditoria | Endpoints misturados (confusão, auditoria difícil) |
| SLO de durabilidade 99.9% | Realista para ~500 registros/mês | 99.999% (1 registro esgota budget anual) |

---

## Itens em Aberto

| Item | Responsável | Status | Criticidade |
|------|-------------|--------|-------------|
| Confirmação de existência de VPC endpoint | Time de Plataforma | Pendente | Média |
| Verificação de idempotência do CBILL | Time CBILL | Pendente | **BLOQUEANTE** |
| Confirmar independência de ordem das promoções | Analista de Negócios | Confirmado | - |
| Definir endpoint de health check do CBILL | Time CBILL | Pendente | Baixa |
| Validar período de retenção de 6 anos com jurídico | Jurídico | Pendente | Alta |
| Implementar callback de sucesso via EventBridge na FeeExemption | Squad Fênix | Pendente | Alta |
| Implementar idempotency_key no payload para CBILL | Squad Fênix | Pendente | **BLOQUEANTE** |
| Confirmar capacidade de CBILL rejeitar duplicatas | Time CBILL | Pendente | **BLOQUEANTE** |

**Nota sobre Itens Bloqueantes**:
Os itens marcados como BLOQUEANTE devem ser resolvidos ANTES de qualquer implementação. Sem idempotência confirmada no CBILL, replay pode causar duplicação de promoções com impacto financeiro.

---

## Alterações V6 → V7

| Aspecto | V6 | V7 |
|---------|----|----|
| Tokenização | SHA-256 com salt | HMAC-SHA256 com chave secreta |
| Armazenamento de chave | SSM | Secrets Manager |
| Callback de sucesso | Não especificado | FeeExemption atualiza DynamoDB |
| Direitos LGPD | Não especificado | Endpoints DELETE e EXPORT |
| Circuit breaker | Todas as falhas | Apenas falhas INFRASTRUCTURE |
| Fan-out de streams | DynamoDB Streams direto | EventBridge Pipes |
| Controle de concorrência | Não especificado | processing_lock_expires_at |
| Backoff exponencial | Não especificado | Fórmula: min(retry^2 × 5min + jitter, 6h) |
| Proteção TTL | Não especificado | archive_status + alarme |
| Retry em conflitos | Não especificado | Backoff para ConditionalCheckFailedException |
| Mascaramento de logs | Apenas mencionado | CloudWatch Data Protection Policy |
| Custo Secrets Manager | N/A | +$0.40/mês |

---

*Versão do Documento: 7.0*
*Última Atualização: 2026-02-04*
*Autores: Revisão de Arquitetura (REV007)*
*Status: Correções de gaps identificados nas revisões V5/V6*

content.content:
1→# Solution V5: CPF Queue Automation for Fee Exemption Reprocessing
     2→
     3→> Architecture Design Document - Revision 6 (Architecture-Focused)
     4→
     5→## Revision History
     6→
     7→| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
     8→|---------|------|--------|---------|
     9→| V0 | 2026-02-03 | Initial | Original proposal |
    10→| V1 | 2026-02-03 | REV002 | Detailed architecture |
    11→| V2 | 2026-02-04 | REV002 | Security, SRE, DevOps fixes |
    12→| V3 | 2026-02-04 | REV003 | Production readiness, DR, SLOs, Testing |
    13→| V4 | 2026-02-04 | REV004 | Production hardening, critical issue fixes |
    14→| V5 | 2026-02-04 | REV005 | Architecture-focused: removed implementation details |
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Executive Summary
    19→
    20→This document describes the **architecture solution** for automating the reprocessing of failed fee exemption postings. V5 is a refinement of V4 that **removes implementation-level details** (code, IAM policies, CloudFormation) to focus on architectural decisions. Implementation details will be addressed in the development phase.
    21→
    22→### Problem Statement
    23→
    24→When fee exemption promotions fail integration with CBILL (on-premises financial system), the current process requires:
    25→1. Manual identification of failed CPFs from DLQ
    26→2. Opening a ticket to the CBILL team for reprocessing
    27→3. No visibility into failure patterns or root causes
    28→
    29→**Constraints**:
    30→- DLQ has 14-day TTL - messages lost after this period
    31→- CBILL is on-premises with limited availability guarantees
    32→- CPF is PII requiring LGPD-compliant handling
    33→
    34→### Solution Overview
    35→
    36→Introduce a **persistent failure store** with **selective replay capability** and **automated retry scheduling**, eliminating manual intervention for common failure scenarios.
    37→
    38→---
    39→
    40→## Architecture Diagram
    41→
    42→```
    43→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    44→│                              CURRENT FLOW                                        │
    45→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    46→│                                                                                  │
    47→│   Data Lake     SQS Main Queue      FeeExemption       CBILL                    │
    48→│   (Afinz)  ───► Isenção_mensalidade ───► Lambda    ───► (on-premises)           │
    49→│                         │                   │                                    │
    50→│                         │              [On Error]                                │
    51→│                         │                   │                                    │
    52→│                         ▼                   ▼                                    │
    53→│                    SQS DLQ ◄────────── Retry X times                            │
    54→│                         │                                                        │
    55→│                         ▼                                                        │
    56→│                  Step Function ───► Email to Squad Fênix                        │
    57→│                                                                                  │
    58→│                  ❌ PROBLEM: Manual reprocessing via CBILL ticket               │
    59→│                                                                                  │
    60→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    61→
    62→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    63→│                              PROPOSED FLOW                                       │
    64→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    65→│                                                                                  │
    66→│   Data Lake     SQS Main Queue      FeeExemption       CBILL                    │
    67→│   (Afinz)  ───► Isenção_mensalidade ───► Lambda    ───► (on-premises)           │
    68→│                         │                   │                                    │
    69→│                         │              [On Error]                                │
    70→│                         │                   │                                    │
    71→│                         ▼                   ▼                                    │
    72→│                    SQS DLQ ◄────────── Retry X times                            │
    73→│                         │                                                        │
    74→│            ┌────────────┴────────────┐                                          │
    75→│            ▼                         ▼                                          │
    76→│     DLQ-Consumer              Step Function                                     │
    77→│       Lambda                 (Email remains)                                    │
    78→│            │                                                                     │
    79→│            ▼                                                                     │
    80→│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐                   │
    81→│   │              DynamoDB - Failure Store                    │                   │
    82→│   │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │                   │
    83→│   │  │ PK: cpf_token (SHA-256 hash)                     │   │                   │
    84→│   │  │ cpf_encrypted: KMS-encrypted CPF                 │   │                   │
    85→│   │  │ status: PENDING_RETRY | IN_PROGRESS | RESOLVED   │   │                   │
    86→│   │  │ error_code, retry_count, created_at, ttl         │   │                   │
    87→│   │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │                   │
    88→│   │                         │                                │                   │
    89→│   │  GSI: status_shard-created_at (for querying by status)  │                   │
    90→│   └─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘                   │
    91→│                             │                                                    │
    92→│            ┌────────────────┼────────────────┐                                  │
    93→│            │                │                │                                  │
    94→│            ▼                ▼                ▼                                  │
    95→│     EventBridge         Private API      DynamoDB                              │
    96→│     (Scheduled)         (Manual)         Streams                               │
    97→│            │                │                │                                  │
    98→│            ▼                ▼                │                                  │
    99→│   ┌─────────────────────────────────┐       │                                  │
   100→│   │        Replay Lambda            │       │                                  │
   101→│   │  (Circuit Breaker Protected)    │◄──────┘                                  │
   102→│   └───────────────┬─────────────────┘       │                                  │
   103→│                   │                         │                                  │
   104→│                   ▼                         ▼                                  │
   105→│           SQS Main Queue             TTL Archiver                              │
   106→│           (Re-inject)                     │                                    │
   107→│                                           ▼                                    │
   108→│                                    S3 Archive                                  │
   109→│                                    (6-year retention)                          │
   110→│                                                                                  │
   111→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   112→```
   113→
   114→---
   115→
   116→## Core Components
   117→
   118→### 1. DLQ-Consumer Lambda
   119→
   120→**Purpose**: Consume messages from DLQ and persist to DynamoDB failure store.
   121→
   122→**Trigger**: SQS Event Source Mapping from `SQS-Isenção_mensalidade_DLQ`
   123→
   124→**Key Behaviors**:
   125→- Tokenizes CPF using SHA-256 + salt (LGPD compliance)
   126→- Encrypts original CPF with KMS before storage
   127→- Performs atomic writes with conditional expressions to prevent duplicates
   128→- Reports batch item failures for partial batch handling
   129→
   130→**Interactions**:
   131→- Reads from: SQS DLQ
   132→- Writes to: DynamoDB Failure Store
   133→- Uses: KMS (encryption), SSM (salt retrieval)
   134→
   135→### 2. DynamoDB Failure Store
   136→
   137→**Purpose**: Persistent store for failed messages, queryable by CPF or status.
   138→
   139→**Table Design**:
   140→
   141→| Attribute | Type | Description |
   142→|-----------|------|-------------|
   143→| `cpf_token` (PK) | String | SHA-256 hash of CPF with salt prefix `v{N}:hash` |
   144→| `created_at` (SK) | String | ISO timestamp for multiple failures per CPF |
   145→| `cpf_encrypted` | Binary | KMS-encrypted original CPF |
   146→| `status` | String | PENDING_RETRY, IN_PROGRESS, RESOLVED, FAILED_PERMANENT |
   147→| `status_shard` | String | `{status}#{shard}` for GSI partitioning |
   148→| `error_code` | String | Original error from CBILL |
   149→| `error_message` | String | Detailed error description |
   150→| `retry_count` | Number | Number of automated retries |
   151→| `next_retry_at` | String | Scheduled next retry timestamp |
   152→| `version` | Number | Optimistic locking version |
   153→| `ttl` | Number | Auto-deletion after 90 days |
   154→
   155→**Global Secondary Index (GSI)**:
   156→- Name: `status-created_at-index`
   157→- PK: `status_shard`
   158→- SK: `created_at`
   159→- Purpose: Query records by status for batch replay
   160→
   161→**Design Decisions**:
   162→- **Salt-versioned tokens** (`v1:hash`) enable salt rotation without orphaning records
   163→- **Sharded status key** prevents GSI hot partitions
   164→- **TTL for auto-cleanup** with archive before deletion
   165→
   166→### 3. Replay Lambda
   167→
   168→**Purpose**: Re-inject failed messages back to the main processing queue.
   169→
   170→**Triggers**:
   171→1. **Scheduled**: EventBridge rule every 15 minutes
   172→2. **Manual**: API Gateway endpoint for selective replay
   173→3. **Stream**: DynamoDB Streams for immediate retry of specific patterns
   174→
   175→**Key Behaviors**:
   176→- Queries DynamoDB for PENDING_RETRY records with `next_retry_at < now()`
   177→- Checks circuit breaker state before processing
   178→- Decrypts CPF using KMS
   179→- Sends message to main SQS queue
   180→- Updates record status to IN_PROGRESS with optimistic locking
   181→- Implements exponential backoff for retry scheduling
   182→
   183→**Interactions**:
   184→- Reads from: DynamoDB Failure Store, Circuit Breaker Table
   185→- Writes to: SQS Main Queue, DynamoDB (status updates)
   186→- Uses: KMS (decryption)
   187→
   188→### 4. Circuit Breaker
   189→
   190→**Purpose**: Protect system from cascading failures when CBILL is unavailable.
   191→
   192→**State Machine**:
   193→```
   194→           5 consecutive          1 min timeout
   195→           failures               (configurable)
   196→    ┌─────────────────┐      ┌─────────────────┐
   197→    │                 │      │                 │
   198→    ▼                 │      ▼                 │
   199→ CLOSED ──────────► OPEN ──────────► HALF_OPEN
   200→    ▲                               │    │
   201→    │                               │    │
   202→    └───────── 3 successes ─────────┘    │
   203→              in HALF_OPEN               │
   204→                                         │
   205→    ┌────────── 1 failure ───────────────┘
   206→    ▼
   207→  OPEN
   208→```
   209→
   210→**Storage**: Dedicated DynamoDB table (not SSM) for:
   211→- Atomic state transitions via conditional writes
   212→- No race conditions between Lambda invocations
   213→- Persistent state (no TTL to prevent silent reset)
   214→- Encryption at rest with KMS
   215→
   216→**States**:
   217→- **CLOSED**: Normal operation, all requests allowed
   218→- **OPEN**: CBILL unavailable, all replay requests blocked
   219→- **HALF_OPEN**: Testing recovery, limited requests allowed
   220→
   221→### 5. Stuck Recovery Lambda
   222→
   223→**Purpose**: Detect and reset records stuck in IN_PROGRESS state.
   224→
   225→**Trigger**: EventBridge schedule every 15 minutes
   226→
   227→**Key Behaviors**:
   228→- Queries GSI for IN_PROGRESS records older than 30 minutes
   229→- Resets status to PENDING_RETRY
   230→- Increments retry count
   231→- Emits CloudWatch metric for monitoring
   232→
   233→### 6. TTL Archiver Lambda
   234→
   235→**Purpose**: Archive records before TTL deletion for LGPD compliance (6-year retention).
   236→
   237→**Trigger**: DynamoDB Streams (REMOVE events only)
   238→
   239→**Key Behaviors**:
   240→- Filters for TTL-initiated deletions only
   241→- Writes record to S3 with Hive-partitioned keys
   242→- Implements retry with DLQ for write failures
   243→- Maintains audit trail for compliance
   244→
   245→**S3 Key Format**: `archives/year={Y}/month={M}/day={D}/{cpf_token}.json`
   246→
   247→### 7. S3 Backup Lambda
   248→
   249→**Purpose**: Real-time backup to S3 for disaster recovery.
   250→
   251→**Trigger**: DynamoDB Streams (INSERT and MODIFY events)
   252→
   253→**Key Behaviors**:
   254→- Exports new/modified records to S3 in near real-time
   255→- Uses version-based keys for idempotency: `backups/{date}/{cpf_token}_v{version}.json`
   256→- Enables cross-region replication for DR
   257→- Does not process DELETE events (handled by TTL Archiver)
   258→
   259→### 8. Health Check Lambda
   260→
   261→**Purpose**: Provide system health status for monitoring and alerting.
   262→
   263→**Trigger**: API Gateway endpoint, CloudWatch Synthetic Canary
   264→
   265→**Components Checked**:
   266→- DynamoDB table accessibility
   267→- SQS queue availability
   268→- CBILL connectivity (lightweight probe)
   269→- Circuit breaker state
   270→
   271→**Response Format**:
   272→```json
   273→{
   274→  "status": "healthy|degraded|unhealthy",
   275→  "components": {
   276→    "dynamodb": {"status": "healthy", "latencyMs": 15},
   277→    "sqs": {"status": "healthy", "latencyMs": 8},
   278→    "cbill": {"status": "unhealthy", "latencyMs": 5000},
   279→    "circuitBreaker": {"state": "OPEN", "failureCount": 5}
   280→  },
   281→  "metrics": {
   282→    "pendingRetryCount": 42,
   283→    "inProgressCount": 3,
   284→    "lastHourFailures": 12
   285→  }
   286→}
   287→```
   288→
   289→### 9. Private API Gateway
   290→
   291→**Purpose**: Manual replay interface for Squad Fênix.
   292→
   293→**Endpoints**:
   294→
   295→| Method | Path | Description |
   296→|--------|------|-------------|
   297→| GET | `/v1/failures` | List failures with pagination and filters |
   298→| GET | `/v1/failures/{cpf}` | Get failures for specific CPF |
   299→| POST | `/v1/replay` | Trigger replay for specific CPF(s) or all pending |
   300→| GET | `/v1/health` | System health check |
   301→
   302→**Security**:
   303→- Private API (VPC endpoint only)
   304→- IAM authentication
   305→- Resource policy restricting to VPC
   306→- Rate limiting via usage plans
   307→
   308→---
   309→
   310→## Data Flow Patterns
   311→
   312→### Pattern 1: Automated Retry (Happy Path)
   313→
   314→```
   315→1. Message fails in FeeExemption Lambda after X retries
   316→2. Message lands in DLQ
   317→3. DLQ-Consumer persists to DynamoDB with status=PENDING_RETRY, next_retry_at=now+15min
   318→4. EventBridge triggers Replay Lambda
   319→5. Replay Lambda queries PENDING_RETRY records where next_retry_at < now
   320→6. Circuit breaker check passes (CLOSED state)
   321→7. CPF decrypted, message sent to main queue
   322→8. Status updated to IN_PROGRESS
   323→9. FeeExemption processes successfully
   324→10. (External) Success callback updates status to RESOLVED
   325→```
   326→
   327→### Pattern 2: Circuit Breaker Protection
   328→
   329→```
   330→1. CBILL becomes unavailable
   331→2. FeeExemption failures accumulate (5+ consecutive)
   332→3. Circuit breaker transitions to OPEN
   333→4. Replay Lambda skips processing, emits metric
   334→5. After 1 minute, circuit breaker transitions to HALF_OPEN
   335→6. Limited test requests allowed
   336→7. 3 consecutive successes → CLOSED
   337→8. OR 1 failure → OPEN again
   338→```
   339→
   340→### Pattern 3: Manual Selective Replay
   341→
   342→```
   343→1. Operator identifies specific CPF(s) to replay
   344→2. POST /v1/replay with cpf list
   345→3. Replay Lambda bypasses schedule, processes immediately
   346→4. Circuit breaker still enforced (can be overridden in emergency)
   347→5. Response includes success/failure for each CPF
   348→```
   349→
   350→### Pattern 4: Data Lifecycle
   351→
   352→```
   353→Day 0:   Record created (status=PENDING_RETRY)
   354→Day 1-7: Automated retries with exponential backoff
   355→Day 7+:  Manual intervention if still failing
   356→Day 90:  TTL triggers deletion
   357→         → DynamoDB Stream → TTL Archiver → S3 Archive
   358→Year 6:  S3 Glacier Deep Archive auto-expires (LGPD compliance)
   359→```
   360→
   361→---
   362→
   363→## Security Architecture
   364→
   365→### CPF Protection (LGPD Compliance)
   366→
   367→**Tokenization Strategy**:
   368→- CPF never stored in plaintext
   369→- SHA-256 HMAC with rotating salt creates `cpf_token`
   370→- Salt versioning (`v1:hash`) enables rotation without data loss
   371→- Original CPF encrypted with KMS before storage
   372→
   373→**Key Management**:
   374→- Dedicated KMS key for CPF encryption
   375→- Multi-region key for DR scenarios
   376→- Key rotation enabled (annual)
   377→- MFA required for key administration
   378→
   379→### Network Security
   380→
   381→- All Lambdas run in VPC (same as FeeExemption)
   382→- Private API Gateway (VPC endpoint only)
   383→- No public internet exposure
   384→- Security groups restrict egress to necessary endpoints only
   385→
   386→### Access Control
   387→
   388→- IAM roles with least-privilege per Lambda
   389→- No wildcard permissions in production
   390→- Cross-region IAM for DR Lambdas explicitly defined
   391→- API Gateway authentication via IAM
   392→
   393→---
   394→
   395→## Disaster Recovery
   396→
   397→### Strategy: Data Durability (Not Processing Continuity)
   398→
   399→**Rationale**: CBILL is on-premises in a single location. If sa-east-1 fails, CBILL is likely also affected. DR focus is on **preserving data** until primary region recovers.
   400→
   401→### Components
   402→
   403→1. **S3 Cross-Region Replication**
   404→   - Source: `fee-exemption-backup-{account}-{env}` (sa-east-1)
   405→   - Target: `fee-exemption-backup-{account}-{env}-dr` (us-east-1)
   406→   - RPO: < 15 minutes (replication SLA)
   407→   - RTO: < 30 minutes (restore procedure)
   408→
   409→2. **Multi-Region KMS Key**
   410→   - Single logical key, replicated across regions
   411→   - DR Lambdas can decrypt without key synchronization
   412→
   413→3. **SSM Parameter Replication**
   414→   - Salt values replicated to DR region
   415→   - EventBridge rule triggers replication on change
   416→
   417→### What DR Does NOT Include
   418→
   419→- Active processing in secondary region (requires multi-region CBILL)
   420→- Global Tables (cost/complexity disproportionate for 48h SLA)
   421→- Active-active architecture
   422→
   423→### Restore Procedure (High-Level)
   424→
   425→1. Create new DynamoDB table in DR region
   426→2. Import from S3 backup (Lambda or AWS Data Pipeline)
   427→3. Update application configuration to use restored table
   428→4. Resume processing when CBILL available
   429→
   430→---
   431→
   432→## Observability
   433→
   434→### CloudWatch Alarms
   435→
   436→| Alarm | Threshold | Action |
   437→|-------|-----------|--------|
   438→| DLQ-Consumer Errors | > 5 in 5 min | Alert Squad Fênix |
   439→| Replay Lambda Errors | > 10 in 15 min | Alert + check CBILL |
   440→| Circuit Breaker OPEN | State = OPEN | Page on-call |
   441→| Stuck IN_PROGRESS Records | > 10 for 1 hour | Alert for investigation |
   442→| TTL Archiver DLQ Messages | > 0 | Critical - potential data loss |
   443→| DynamoDB Throttling | > 0 | Scale or optimize |
   444→
   445→### CloudWatch Dashboard
   446→
   447→Sections:
   448→1. **Queue Health**: DLQ depth, main queue age
   449→2. **Failure Store**: Records by status, daily trend
   450→3. **Replay Metrics**: Success rate, latency p95
   451→4. **Circuit Breaker**: Current state, transition history
   452→5. **System Health**: Lambda errors, throttles, duration
   453→
   454→### Logging
   455→
   456→- Structured JSON format
   457→- CPF masked in logs (show last 4 digits only)
   458→- Trace ID propagation (X-Ray)
   459→- Correlation ID for cross-service tracking
   460→
   461→---
   462→
   463→## SLOs and Error Budgets
   464→
   465→| SLO | Target | Error Budget (Monthly) |
   466→|-----|--------|------------------------|
   467→| Availability | 99.9% | 43.8 min downtime |
   468→| Data Durability | 99.9% | 1 lost record per 1000 |
   469→| Replay Latency | p95 < 10 min | 5% slow replays |
   470→| Time to Resolution | < 48 hours | 5% exceed SLA |
   471→| Successful Replay Rate | > 95% | 5% permanent failures |
   472→
   473→---
   474→
   475→## Cost Estimate
   476→
   477→### Monthly Cost (Production, ~500 records/month)
   478→
   479→| Component | Estimated Cost |
   480→|-----------|----------------|
   481→| DynamoDB (on-demand) | $2.50 |
   482→| Lambda (5 functions) | $0.20 |
   483→| API Gateway (private) | $0.70 |
   484→| KMS (1 multi-region key) | $1.03 |
   485→| S3 (archive + backup) | $0.15 |
   486→| CloudWatch (logs + metrics + alarms) | $3.50 |
   487→| EventBridge | $0.00 |
   488→| Circuit Breaker Table | $0.50 |
   489→| **Subtotal** | **~$8.58** |
   490→| VPC Endpoints* | $0 - $21.60 |
   491→| **Total** | **$8.58 - $30.18** |
   492→
   493→*VPC Endpoints: $0 if already exist for other services; $21.60 if new endpoints required. Confirm with platform team.
   494→
   495→---
   496→
   497→## Implementation Phases
   498→
   499→### Phase 0: Prerequisites
   500→- Confirm VPC endpoint availability
   501→- Create KMS key with multi-region configuration
   502→- Set up SSM parameters for salt and configuration
   503→- Create Circuit Breaker DynamoDB table
   504→
   505→### Phase 1: Core Components
   506→- Deploy DLQ-Consumer Lambda
   507→- Create DynamoDB Failure Store with GSI
   508→- Deploy Replay Lambda with circuit breaker integration
   509→- Configure EventBridge schedule
   510→
   511→### Phase 2: Reliability & Operations
   512→- Deploy Stuck Recovery Lambda
   513→- Deploy TTL Archiver with DLQ
   514→- Deploy S3 Backup Lambda with cross-region replication
   515→- Configure CloudWatch alarms and dashboard
   516→
   517→### Phase 3: API & Observability
   518→- Deploy Private API Gateway
   519→- Deploy Health Check Lambda
   520→- Enable X-Ray tracing
   521→- Create operational runbooks
   522→
   523→### Phase 4: Validation
   524→- Integration testing with test CPFs
   525→- Chaos testing (FIS experiments)
   526→- Runbook exercises with Squad Fênix
   527→- Canary deployment (1% traffic, 48h observation)
   528→
   529→---
   530→
   531→## Operational Runbooks (Summary)
   532→
   533→| ID | Runbook | Trigger |
   534→|----|---------|---------|
   535→| RB-001 | Manual Replay by CPF | Support request for specific CPF |
   536→| RB-002 | Mass Replay After CBILL Fix | CBILL issue resolved |
   537→| RB-003 | Circuit Breaker Investigation | CB OPEN alarm |
   538→| RB-004 | Stuck Records Recovery | Stuck alarm |
   539→| RB-005 | DLQ-Consumer Failure Investigation | Consumer error alarm |
   540→| RB-006 | TTL Archiver Failure Recovery | Archiver DLQ alarm |
   541→| RB-007 | DynamoDB Capacity Scaling | Throttle alarm |
   542→| RB-008 | Salt Rotation | Annual or compromise |
   543→| RB-009 | Circuit Breaker Manual Override | Emergency replay needed |
   544→| RB-010 | Health Check Failure Investigation | Health endpoint returns unhealthy |
   545→| RB-011 | DR Restore from S3 Backup | Primary region failure |
   546→
   547→---
   548→
   549→## Decision Log
   550→
   551→| Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Rejected |
   552→|----------|-----------|----------------------|
   553→| DynamoDB over RDS | Serverless, pay-per-use, simple KV pattern | RDS (over-provisioned), Redis (no durability) |
   554→| SHA-256 tokenization | Industry standard, irreversible | Base64 (reversible), bcrypt (slower) |
   555→| Salt versioning | Enables rotation without data loss | Single salt (rotation = orphaned records) |
   556→| DynamoDB circuit breaker | Atomic operations, persistent | SSM (race conditions), in-memory (stateless) |
   557→| S3 backup over Global Tables | Simpler, cheaper, adequate RPO for 48h SLA | Global Tables (2x cost, unnecessary complexity) |
   558→| Private API over public | Internal use only, security | Public + WAF (unnecessary cost) |
   559→| Remove WAF | Private API in VPC, low traffic | Keep WAF ($5.50/month for 200 requests) |
   560→| 99.9% durability SLO | Realistic for ~500 records/month | 99.999% (1 record exhausts annual budget) |
   561→
   562→---
   563→
   564→## Open Items
   565→
   566→| Item | Owner | Status |
   567→|------|-------|--------|
   568→| VPC endpoint existence confirmation | Platform Team | Pending |
   569→| CBILL idempotency verification | CBILL Team | Pending |
   570→| Confirm promotion order independence | Business Analyst | Confirmed |
   571→| Define CBILL health check endpoint | CBILL Team | Pending |
   572→
   573→---
   574→
   575→*Document Version: 5.0*
   576→*Last Updated: 2026-02-04*
   577→*Authors: Architecture Review (REV005)*
   578→*Status: Architecture-focused revision (implementation details removed)*
   579→

# Solution V5: CPF Queue Automation for Fee Exemption Reprocessing

> Architecture Design Document - Revision 6 (Architecture-Focused)

## Revision History

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| V0 | 2026-02-03 | Initial | Original proposal |
| V1 | 2026-02-03 | REV002 | Detailed architecture |
| V2 | 2026-02-04 | REV002 | Security, SRE, DevOps fixes |
| V3 | 2026-02-04 | REV003 | Production readiness, DR, SLOs, Testing |
| V4 | 2026-02-04 | REV004 | Production hardening, critical issue fixes |
| V5 | 2026-02-04 | REV005 | Architecture-focused: removed implementation details |

---

## Executive Summary

This document describes the **architecture solution** for automating the reprocessing of failed fee exemption postings. V5 is a refinement of V4 that **removes implementation-level details** (code, IAM policies, CloudFormation) to focus on architectural decisions. Implementation details will be addressed in the development phase.

### Problem Statement

When fee exemption promotions fail integration with CBILL (on-premises financial system), the current process requires:
1. Manual identification of failed CPFs from DLQ
2. Opening a ticket to the CBILL team for reprocessing
3. No visibility into failure patterns or root causes

**Constraints**:
- DLQ has 14-day TTL - messages lost after this period
- CBILL is on-premises with limited availability guarantees
- CPF is PII requiring LGPD-compliant handling

### Solution Overview

Introduce a **persistent failure store** with **selective replay capability** and **automated retry scheduling**, eliminating manual intervention for common failure scenarios.

---

## Architecture Diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                              CURRENT FLOW                                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                  │
│   Data Lake     SQS Main Queue      FeeExemption       CBILL                    │
│   (Afinz)  ───► Isenção_mensalidade ───► Lambda    ───► (on-premises)           │
│                         │                   │                                    │
│                         │              [On Error]                                │
│                         │                   │                                    │
│                         ▼                   ▼                                    │
│                    SQS DLQ ◄────────── Retry X times                            │
│                         │                                                        │
│                         ▼                                                        │
│                  Step Function ───► Email to Squad Fênix                        │
│                                                                                  │
│                  ❌ PROBLEM: Manual reprocessing via CBILL ticket               │
│                                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                              PROPOSED FLOW                                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                                  │
│   Data Lake     SQS Main Queue      FeeExemption       CBILL                    │
│   (Afinz)  ───► Isenção_mensalidade ───► Lambda    ───► (on-premises)           │
│                         │                   │                                    │
│                         │              [On Error]                                │
│                         │                   │                                    │
│                         ▼                   ▼                                    │
│                    SQS DLQ ◄────────── Retry X times                            │
│                         │                                                        │
│            ┌────────────┴────────────┐                                          │
│            ▼                         ▼                                          │
│     DLQ-Consumer              Step Function                                     │
│       Lambda                 (Email remains)                                    │
│            │                                                                     │
│            ▼                                                                     │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐                   │
│   │              DynamoDB - Failure Store                    │                   │
│   │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │                   │
│   │  │ PK: cpf_token (SHA-256 hash)                     │   │                   │
│   │  │ cpf_encrypted: KMS-encrypted CPF                 │   │                   │
│   │  │ status: PENDING_RETRY | IN_PROGRESS | RESOLVED   │   │                   │
│   │  │ error_code, retry_count, created_at, ttl         │   │                   │
│   │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │                   │
│   │                         │                                │                   │
│   │  GSI: status_shard-created_at (for querying by status)  │                   │
│   └─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘                   │
│                             │                                                    │
│            ┌────────────────┼────────────────┐                                  │
│            │                │                │                                  │
│            ▼                ▼                ▼                                  │
│     EventBridge         Private API      DynamoDB                              │
│     (Scheduled)         (Manual)         Streams                               │
│            │                │                │                                  │
│            ▼                ▼                │                                  │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────┐       │                                  │
│   │        Replay Lambda            │       │                                  │
│   │  (Circuit Breaker Protected)    │◄──────┘                                  │
│   └───────────────┬─────────────────┘       │                                  │
│                   │                         │                                  │
│                   ▼                         ▼                                  │
│           SQS Main Queue             TTL Archiver                              │
│           (Re-inject)                     │                                    │
│                                           ▼                                    │
│                                    S3 Archive                                  │
│                                    (6-year retention)                          │
│                                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Core Components

### 1. DLQ-Consumer Lambda

**Purpose**: Consume messages from DLQ and persist to DynamoDB failure store.

**Trigger**: SQS Event Source Mapping from `SQS-Isenção_mensalidade_DLQ`

**Key Behaviors**:
- Tokenizes CPF using SHA-256 + salt (LGPD compliance)
- Encrypts original CPF with KMS before storage
- Performs atomic writes with conditional expressions to prevent duplicates
- Reports batch item failures for partial batch handling

**Interactions**:
- Reads from: SQS DLQ
- Writes to: DynamoDB Failure Store
- Uses: KMS (encryption), SSM (salt retrieval)

### 2. DynamoDB Failure Store

**Purpose**: Persistent store for failed messages, queryable by CPF or status.

**Table Design**:

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `cpf_token` (PK) | String | SHA-256 hash of CPF with salt prefix `v{N}:hash` |
| `created_at` (SK) | String | ISO timestamp for multiple failures per CPF |
| `cpf_encrypted` | Binary | KMS-encrypted original CPF |
| `status` | String | PENDING_RETRY, IN_PROGRESS, RESOLVED, FAILED_PERMANENT |
| `status_shard` | String | `{status}#{shard}` for GSI partitioning |
| `error_code` | String | Original error from CBILL |
| `error_message` | String | Detailed error description |
| `retry_count` | Number | Number of automated retries |
| `next_retry_at` | String | Scheduled next retry timestamp |
| `version` | Number | Optimistic locking version |
| `ttl` | Number | Auto-deletion after 90 days |

**Global Secondary Index (GSI)**:
- Name: `status-created_at-index`
- PK: `status_shard`
- SK: `created_at`
- Purpose: Query records by status for batch replay

**Design Decisions**:
- **Salt-versioned tokens** (`v1:hash`) enable salt rotation without orphaning records
- **Sharded status key** prevents GSI hot partitions
- **TTL for auto-cleanup** with archive before deletion

### 3. Replay Lambda

**Purpose**: Re-inject failed messages back to the main processing queue.

**Triggers**:
1. **Scheduled**: EventBridge rule every 15 minutes
2. **Manual**: API Gateway endpoint for selective replay
3. **Stream**: DynamoDB Streams for immediate retry of specific patterns

**Key Behaviors**:
- Queries DynamoDB for PENDING_RETRY records with `next_retry_at < now()`
- Checks circuit breaker state before processing
- Decrypts CPF using KMS
- Sends message to main SQS queue
- Updates record status to IN_PROGRESS with optimistic locking
- Implements exponential backoff for retry scheduling

**Interactions**:
- Reads from: DynamoDB Failure Store, Circuit Breaker Table
- Writes to: SQS Main Queue, DynamoDB (status updates)
- Uses: KMS (decryption)

### 4. Circuit Breaker

**Purpose**: Protect system from cascading failures when CBILL is unavailable.

**State Machine**:
```
           5 consecutive          1 min timeout
           failures               (configurable)
    ┌─────────────────┐      ┌─────────────────┐
    │                 │      │                 │
    ▼                 │      ▼                 │
 CLOSED ──────────► OPEN ──────────► HALF_OPEN
    ▲                               │    │
    │                               │    │
    └───────── 3 successes ─────────┘    │
              in HALF_OPEN               │
                                         │
    ┌────────── 1 failure ───────────────┘
    ▼
  OPEN
```

**Storage**: Dedicated DynamoDB table (not SSM) for:
- Atomic state transitions via conditional writes
- No race conditions between Lambda invocations
- Persistent state (no TTL to prevent silent reset)
- Encryption at rest with KMS

**States**:
- **CLOSED**: Normal operation, all requests allowed
- **OPEN**: CBILL unavailable, all replay requests blocked
- **HALF_OPEN**: Testing recovery, limited requests allowed

### 5. Stuck Recovery Lambda

**Purpose**: Detect and reset records stuck in IN_PROGRESS state.

**Trigger**: EventBridge schedule every 15 minutes

**Key Behaviors**:
- Queries GSI for IN_PROGRESS records older than 30 minutes
- Resets status to PENDING_RETRY
- Increments retry count
- Emits CloudWatch metric for monitoring

### 6. TTL Archiver Lambda

**Purpose**: Archive records before TTL deletion for LGPD compliance (6-year retention).

**Trigger**: DynamoDB Streams (REMOVE events only)

**Key Behaviors**:
- Filters for TTL-initiated deletions only
- Writes record to S3 with Hive-partitioned keys
- Implements retry with DLQ for write failures
- Maintains audit trail for compliance

**S3 Key Format**: `archives/year={Y}/month={M}/day={D}/{cpf_token}.json`

### 7. S3 Backup Lambda

**Purpose**: Real-time backup to S3 for disaster recovery.

**Trigger**: DynamoDB Streams (INSERT and MODIFY events)

**Key Behaviors**:
- Exports new/modified records to S3 in near real-time
- Uses version-based keys for idempotency: `backups/{date}/{cpf_token}_v{version}.json`
- Enables cross-region replication for DR
- Does not process DELETE events (handled by TTL Archiver)

### 8. Health Check Lambda

**Purpose**: Provide system health status for monitoring and alerting.

**Trigger**: API Gateway endpoint, CloudWatch Synthetic Canary

**Components Checked**:
- DynamoDB table accessibility
- SQS queue availability
- CBILL connectivity (lightweight probe)
- Circuit breaker state

**Response Format**:
```json
{
  "status": "healthy|degraded|unhealthy",
  "components": {
    "dynamodb": {"status": "healthy", "latencyMs": 15},
    "sqs": {"status": "healthy", "latencyMs": 8},
    "cbill": {"status": "unhealthy", "latencyMs": 5000},
    "circuitBreaker": {"state": "OPEN", "failureCount": 5}
  },
  "metrics": {
    "pendingRetryCount": 42,
    "inProgressCount": 3,
    "lastHourFailures": 12
  }
}
```

### 9. Private API Gateway

**Purpose**: Manual replay interface for Squad Fênix.

**Endpoints**:

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/failures` | List failures with pagination and filters |
| GET | `/v1/failures/{cpf}` | Get failures for specific CPF |
| POST | `/v1/replay` | Trigger replay for specific CPF(s) or all pending |
| GET | `/v1/health` | System health check |

**Security**:
- Private API (VPC endpoint only)
- IAM authentication
- Resource policy restricting to VPC
- Rate limiting via usage plans

---

## Data Flow Patterns

### Pattern 1: Automated Retry (Happy Path)

```
1. Message fails in FeeExemption Lambda after X retries
2. Message lands in DLQ
3. DLQ-Consumer persists to DynamoDB with status=PENDING_RETRY, next_retry_at=now+15min
4. EventBridge triggers Replay Lambda
5. Replay Lambda queries PENDING_RETRY records where next_retry_at < now
6. Circuit breaker check passes (CLOSED state)
7. CPF decrypted, message sent to main queue
8. Status updated to IN_PROGRESS
9. FeeExemption processes successfully
10. (External) Success callback updates status to RESOLVED
```

### Pattern 2: Circuit Breaker Protection

```
1. CBILL becomes unavailable
2. FeeExemption failures accumulate (5+ consecutive)
3. Circuit breaker transitions to OPEN
4. Replay Lambda skips processing, emits metric
5. After 1 minute, circuit breaker transitions to HALF_OPEN
6. Limited test requests allowed
7. 3 consecutive successes → CLOSED
8. OR 1 failure → OPEN again
```

### Pattern 3: Manual Selective Replay

```
1. Operator identifies specific CPF(s) to replay
2. POST /v1/replay with cpf list
3. Replay Lambda bypasses schedule, processes immediately
4. Circuit breaker still enforced (can be overridden in emergency)
5. Response includes success/failure for each CPF
```

### Pattern 4: Data Lifecycle

```
Day 0:   Record created (status=PENDING_RETRY)
Day 1-7: Automated retries with exponential backoff
Day 7+:  Manual intervention if still failing
Day 90:  TTL triggers deletion
         → DynamoDB Stream → TTL Archiver → S3 Archive
Year 6:  S3 Glacier Deep Archive auto-expires (LGPD compliance)
```

---

## Security Architecture

### CPF Protection (LGPD Compliance)

**Tokenization Strategy**:
- CPF never stored in plaintext
- SHA-256 HMAC with rotating salt creates `cpf_token`
- Salt versioning (`v1:hash`) enables rotation without data loss
- Original CPF encrypted with KMS before storage

**Key Management**:
- Dedicated KMS key for CPF encryption
- Multi-region key for DR scenarios
- Key rotation enabled (annual)
- MFA required for key administration

### Network Security

- All Lambdas run in VPC (same as FeeExemption)
- Private API Gateway (VPC endpoint only)
- No public internet exposure
- Security groups restrict egress to necessary endpoints only

### Access Control

- IAM roles with least-privilege per Lambda
- No wildcard permissions in production
- Cross-region IAM for DR Lambdas explicitly defined
- API Gateway authentication via IAM

---

## Disaster Recovery

### Strategy: Data Durability (Not Processing Continuity)

**Rationale**: CBILL is on-premises in a single location. If sa-east-1 fails, CBILL is likely also affected. DR focus is on **preserving data** until primary region recovers.

### Components

1. **S3 Cross-Region Replication**
   - Source: `fee-exemption-backup-{account}-{env}` (sa-east-1)
   - Target: `fee-exemption-backup-{account}-{env}-dr` (us-east-1)
   - RPO: < 15 minutes (replication SLA)
   - RTO: < 30 minutes (restore procedure)

2. **Multi-Region KMS Key**
   - Single logical key, replicated across regions
   - DR Lambdas can decrypt without key synchronization

3. **SSM Parameter Replication**
   - Salt values replicated to DR region
   - EventBridge rule triggers replication on change

### What DR Does NOT Include

- Active processing in secondary region (requires multi-region CBILL)
- Global Tables (cost/complexity disproportionate for 48h SLA)
- Active-active architecture

### Restore Procedure (High-Level)

1. Create new DynamoDB table in DR region
2. Import from S3 backup (Lambda or AWS Data Pipeline)
3. Update application configuration to use restored table
4. Resume processing when CBILL available

---

## Observability

### CloudWatch Alarms

| Alarm | Threshold | Action |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| DLQ-Consumer Errors | > 5 in 5 min | Alert Squad Fênix |
| Replay Lambda Errors | > 10 in 15 min | Alert + check CBILL |
| Circuit Breaker OPEN | State = OPEN | Page on-call |
| Stuck IN_PROGRESS Records | > 10 for 1 hour | Alert for investigation |
| TTL Archiver DLQ Messages | > 0 | Critical - potential data loss |
| DynamoDB Throttling | > 0 | Scale or optimize |

### CloudWatch Dashboard

Sections:
1. **Queue Health**: DLQ depth, main queue age
2. **Failure Store**: Records by status, daily trend
3. **Replay Metrics**: Success rate, latency p95
4. **Circuit Breaker**: Current state, transition history
5. **System Health**: Lambda errors, throttles, duration

### Logging

- Structured JSON format
- CPF masked in logs (show last 4 digits only)
- Trace ID propagation (X-Ray)
- Correlation ID for cross-service tracking

---

## SLOs and Error Budgets

| SLO | Target | Error Budget (Monthly) |
|-----|--------|------------------------|
| Availability | 99.9% | 43.8 min downtime |
| Data Durability | 99.9% | 1 lost record per 1000 |
| Replay Latency | p95 < 10 min | 5% slow replays |
| Time to Resolution | < 48 hours | 5% exceed SLA |
| Successful Replay Rate | > 95% | 5% permanent failures |

---

## Cost Estimate

### Monthly Cost (Production, ~500 records/month)

| Component | Estimated Cost |
|-----------|----------------|
| DynamoDB (on-demand) | $2.50 |
| Lambda (5 functions) | $0.20 |
| API Gateway (private) | $0.70 |
| KMS (1 multi-region key) | $1.03 |
| S3 (archive + backup) | $0.15 |
| CloudWatch (logs + metrics + alarms) | $3.50 |
| EventBridge | $0.00 |
| Circuit Breaker Table | $0.50 |
| **Subtotal** | **~$8.58** |
| VPC Endpoints* | $0 - $21.60 |
| **Total** | **$8.58 - $30.18** |

*VPC Endpoints: $0 if already exist for other services; $21.60 if new endpoints required. Confirm with platform team.

---

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 0: Prerequisites
- Confirm VPC endpoint availability
- Create KMS key with multi-region configuration
- Set up SSM parameters for salt and configuration
- Create Circuit Breaker DynamoDB table

### Phase 1: Core Components
- Deploy DLQ-Consumer Lambda
- Create DynamoDB Failure Store with GSI
- Deploy Replay Lambda with circuit breaker integration
- Configure EventBridge schedule

### Phase 2: Reliability & Operations
- Deploy Stuck Recovery Lambda
- Deploy TTL Archiver with DLQ
- Deploy S3 Backup Lambda with cross-region replication
- Configure CloudWatch alarms and dashboard

### Phase 3: API & Observability
- Deploy Private API Gateway
- Deploy Health Check Lambda
- Enable X-Ray tracing
- Create operational runbooks

### Phase 4: Validation
- Integration testing with test CPFs
- Chaos testing (FIS experiments)
- Runbook exercises with Squad Fênix
- Canary deployment (1% traffic, 48h observation)

---

## Operational Runbooks (Summary)

| ID | Runbook | Trigger |
|----|---------|---------|
| RB-001 | Manual Replay by CPF | Support request for specific CPF |
| RB-002 | Mass Replay After CBILL Fix | CBILL issue resolved |
| RB-003 | Circuit Breaker Investigation | CB OPEN alarm |
| RB-004 | Stuck Records Recovery | Stuck alarm |
| RB-005 | DLQ-Consumer Failure Investigation | Consumer error alarm |
| RB-006 | TTL Archiver Failure Recovery | Archiver DLQ alarm |
| RB-007 | DynamoDB Capacity Scaling | Throttle alarm |
| RB-008 | Salt Rotation | Annual or compromise |
| RB-009 | Circuit Breaker Manual Override | Emergency replay needed |
| RB-010 | Health Check Failure Investigation | Health endpoint returns unhealthy |
| RB-011 | DR Restore from S3 Backup | Primary region failure |

---

## Decision Log

| Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Rejected |
|----------|-----------|----------------------|
| DynamoDB over RDS | Serverless, pay-per-use, simple KV pattern | RDS (over-provisioned), Redis (no durability) |
| SHA-256 tokenization | Industry standard, irreversible | Base64 (reversible), bcrypt (slower) |
| Salt versioning | Enables rotation without data loss | Single salt (rotation = orphaned records) |
| DynamoDB circuit breaker | Atomic operations, persistent | SSM (race conditions), in-memory (stateless) |
| S3 backup over Global Tables | Simpler, cheaper, adequate RPO for 48h SLA | Global Tables (2x cost, unnecessary complexity) |
| Private API over public | Internal use only, security | Public + WAF (unnecessary cost) |
| Remove WAF | Private API in VPC, low traffic | Keep WAF ($5.50/month for 200 requests) |
| 99.9% durability SLO | Realistic for ~500 records/month | 99.999% (1 record exhausts annual budget) |

---

## Open Items

| Item | Owner | Status |
|------|-------|--------|
| VPC endpoint existence confirmation | Platform Team | Pending |
| CBILL idempotency verification | CBILL Team | Pending |
| Confirm promotion order independence | Business Analyst | Confirmed |
| Define CBILL health check endpoint | CBILL Team | Pending |

    13→---
    14→
    15→## Executive Summary
    16→
    17→This document presents a hardened architecture for automating the reprocessing of failed fee exemption postings to CBILL. **V2 incorporates critical fixes identified during architecture review**, addressing security vulnerabilities (LGPD compliance), reliability gaps (race conditions, idempotency), and operational readiness (IaC, runbooks, observability).
    18→
    19→### Key Changes from V1
    20→
    21→1. **CPF Tokenization** - CPF no longer stored as plaintext partition key
    22→2. **Atomic Idempotency** - Conditional writes replace check-then-write pattern
    23→3. **Circuit Breaker** - Protection against CBILL degradation
    24→4. **Complete IaC** - Full CloudFormation templates required
    25→5. **VPC Integration** - All Lambdas in same VPC as FeeExemption
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→## Problem Statement
    30→
    31→*(Unchanged from V1)*
    32→
    33→### Current Pain Points
    34→
    35→1. **Manual Reprocessing**: Requires opening tickets with CBILL team
    36→2. **TTL Data Loss**: Messages lost after 14 days in DLQ
    37→3. **No Selective Replay**: Native SQS Redrive moves ALL messages, not specific CPFs
    38→4. **No Audit Trail**: No historical record of failures and retry attempts
    39→5. **Limited Visibility**: Monitoring only via AWS Console
    40→
    41→---
    42→
    43→## Architecture Overview
    44→
    45→### Design Principles
    46→
    47→1. **Durability Over Convenience**: Persist failures immediately, don't rely on DLQ retention
    48→2. **Selective Replay**: Enable CPF-level granularity for reprocessing
    49→3. **Idempotency**: Ensure safe retries without duplicate promotions (MANDATORY)
    50→4. **Observability**: Full audit trail and metrics with distributed tracing
    51→5. **Minimal Disruption**: Extend existing infrastructure, don't replace
    52→6. **LGPD Compliance**: CPF protected with tokenization and encryption
    53→7. **Fail-Safe Operations**: Circuit breakers and graceful degradation
    54→
    55→### High-Level Flow
    56→

   135→
   136→---
   137→
   138→## Component Specifications
   139→
   140→### 1. Lambda: DLQ-Consumer (REVISED)
   141→
   142→**Purpose**: Consume messages from DLQ and persist to DynamoDB before TTL expiration.
   143→
   144→**Configuration**:
   145→- Runtime: Node.js 20.x
   146→- Memory: 256 MB
   147→- Timeout: 30 seconds
   148→- Trigger: SQS Event Source Mapping from `SQS-Isenção_mensalidade_DLQ`
   149→- Batch Size: 10
   150→- **VPC**: Same VPC as FeeExemption Lambda (REQUIRED)
   151→- **FunctionResponseTypes**: `ReportBatchItemFailures` (CRITICAL)
   152→- Concurrency: 5 (reserved)
   153→
   154→**Data Model** (V2 - With Tokenization):
   155→```typescript
   156→interface FailureRecord {
   157→  cpf_token: string;           // PK: SHA-256(cpf + SALT) - for lookups
   158→  created_at: string;          // SK: ISO 8601 + UUID suffix for uniqueness
   159→  cpf_encrypted: string;       // KMS-encrypted CPF for retrieval
   160→  payload: SQSMessageBody;
   161→  error_detail: string;
   162→  error_code: string;
   163→  status: 'PENDING_RETRY' | 'IN_PROGRESS' | 'RESOLVED' | 'EXPIRED' | 'PERMANENT_FAILURE';
   164→  retry_count: number;
   165→  total_retry_count: number;   // NEW: Accumulates across all cycles
   166→  max_retries: number;
   167→  last_retry_at: string | null;
   168→  in_progress_since: string | null;  // NEW: For stuck detection
   169→  source_message_id: string;
   170→  idempotency_key: string;
   171→  trace_id: string;            // NEW: X-Ray trace ID
   172→  ttl: number;
   173→  version: number;             // NEW: Optimistic locking
   174→}
   175→```
   176→
   177→**CRITICAL: Atomic Write Implementation**:
   178→```typescript
   179→// WRONG (V1): Check-then-write pattern
   180→// const existing = await dynamodb.query(...);
   181→// if (!existing) await dynamodb.put(...);
   182→
   183→// CORRECT (V2): Atomic conditional write
   184→const params: PutItemCommandInput = {
   185→  TableName: 'fee_exemption_failures',
   186→  Item: marshall(record),
   187→  ConditionExpression:
   188→    'attribute_not_exists(cpf_token) AND attribute_not_exists(created_at)',
   189→  ReturnValuesOnConditionCheckFailure: 'ALL_OLD'
   190→};
   191→
   192→try {
   193→  await dynamodb.putItem(params);
   194→} catch (error) {
   195→  if (error.name === 'ConditionalCheckFailedException') {
   196→    // Duplicate - log and skip, NOT an error
   197→    logger.info({ idempotencyKey: record.idempotency_key }, 'Duplicate record, skipping');
   198→    return; // Allow SQS message to be deleted
   199→  }
   200→  throw error; // Real error - message will retry
   201→}
   202→```
   203→
   204→**CRITICAL: Batch Failure Handling**:
   205→```typescript
   206→export const handler: SQSHandler = async (event) => {
   207→  const batchItemFailures: SQSBatchItemFailure[] = [];
   208→
   209→  for (const record of event.Records) {
   210→    try {
   211→      await processRecord(record);
   212→    } catch (error) {
   213→      logger.error({ messageId: record.messageId, error }, 'Failed to process record');
   214→      batchItemFailures.push({ itemIdentifier: record.messageId });
   215→    }
   216→  }
   217→
   218→  return { batchItemFailures };
   219→};
   220→```
   221→
   222→**Error Classification Logic**:
   223→```typescript
   224→const classifyError = (errorMessage: string): { code: string; retryable: boolean } => {
   225→  if (errorMessage.includes('TIMEOUT')) return { code: 'CBILL_TIMEOUT', retryable: true };
   226→  if (errorMessage.includes('UNAVAILABLE')) return { code: 'CBILL_UNAVAILABLE', retryable: true };
   227→  if (errorMessage.includes('NRC_NOT_FOUND')) return { code: 'NRC_NOT_FOUND', retryable: false };
   228→  if (errorMessage.includes('ACCESS_POINT_NOT_FOUND')) return { code: 'ACCESS_POINT_NOT_FOUND', retryable: false };
   229→  if (errorMessage.includes('DUPLICATE')) return { code: 'DUPLICATE_PROMOTION', retryable: false };
   230→  if (errorMessage.includes('INVALID_CPF')) return { code: 'INVALID_CPF', retryable: false };
   231→  return { code: 'UNKNOWN', retryable: true };
   232→};
   233→
   234→// Non-retryable errors go directly to PERMANENT_FAILURE
   235→if (!errorClassification.retryable) {
   236→  record.status = 'PERMANENT_FAILURE';
   237→}
   238→```
   239→
   240→### 2. DynamoDB Table: `fee_exemption_failures` (REVISED)
   241→
   242→**Schema (V2 - LGPD Compliant)**:
   243→
   244→| Attribute | Type | Key | Description |
   245→|-----------|------|-----|-------------|
   246→| `cpf_token` | String | PK | SHA-256(CPF + SALT) for secure lookups |
   247→| `created_at` | String | SK | ISO 8601 + UUID suffix (e.g., `2024-01-15T10:30:00Z#a1b2c3d4`) |
   248→| `cpf_encrypted` | Binary | - | KMS-encrypted CPF (AES-256-GCM) |
   249→| `payload` | Map | - | Original SQS message body |
   250→| `error_detail` | String | - | Error message from CBILL |
   251→| `error_code` | String | - | Categorized error code |
   252→| `status` | String | - | Processing status |
   253→| `retry_count` | Number | - | Retries in current cycle |
   254→| `total_retry_count` | Number | - | Total retries across all cycles |
   255→| `max_retries` | Number | - | Maximum allowed retries (default: 10) |
   256→| `last_retry_at` | String | - | Timestamp of last retry |
   257→| `in_progress_since` | String | - | When status changed to IN_PROGRESS |
   258→| `source_message_id` | String | - | Original SQS MessageId |
   259→| `idempotency_key` | String | - | Deduplication key |
   260→| `trace_id` | String | - | X-Ray trace ID for correlation |
   261→| `ttl` | Number | - | DynamoDB TTL (Unix timestamp) |
   262→| `version` | Number | - | Optimistic locking version |
   263→
   264→**Global Secondary Indexes**:
   265→
   266→1. **status-created_at-index** (REVISED for hot partition mitigation)
   267→   - PK: `status_shard` (computed: `${status}#${hash(cpf_token) % 10}`)
   268→   - SK: `created_at`
   269→   - Projection: INCLUDE (`cpf_token`, `retry_count`, `max_retries`, `idempotency_key`)
   270→
   271→2. **idempotency-index**
   272→   - PK: `idempotency_key`
   273→   - Projection: KEYS_ONLY
   274→
   275→**CloudFormation (COMPLETE)**:
   276→```yaml
   277→FeeExemptionFailuresTable:
   278→  Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
   279→  DeletionPolicy: Retain
   280→  UpdateReplacePolicy: Retain
   281→  Properties:
   282→    TableName: !Sub 'fee_exemption_failures_${Environment}'
   283→    BillingMode: PAY_PER_REQUEST
   284→    DeletionProtectionEnabled: true
   285→    AttributeDefinitions:
   286→      - AttributeName: cpf_token
   287→        AttributeType: S
   288→      - AttributeName: created_at
   289→        AttributeType: S
   290→      - AttributeName: status_shard
   291→        AttributeType: S
   292→      - AttributeName: idempotency_key
   293→        AttributeType: S
   294→    KeySchema:
   295→      - AttributeName: cpf_token
   296→        KeyType: HASH
   297→      - AttributeName: created_at
   298→        KeyType: RANGE
   299→    GlobalSecondaryIndexes:
   300→      - IndexName: status-created_at-index
   301→        KeySchema:
   302→          - AttributeName: status_shard
   303→            KeyType: HASH
   304→          - AttributeName: created_at
   305→            KeyType: RANGE
   306→        Projection:
   307→          ProjectionType: INCLUDE
   308→          NonKeyAttributes:
   309→            - cpf_token
   310→            - retry_count
   311→            - max_retries
   312→            - idempotency_key
   313→      - IndexName: idempotency-index
   314→        KeySchema:
   315→          - AttributeName: idempotency_key
   316→            KeyType: HASH
   317→        Projection:
   318→          ProjectionType: KEYS_ONLY
   319→    TimeToLiveSpecification:
   320→      AttributeName: ttl
   321→      Enabled: true
   322→    PointInTimeRecoverySpecification:
   323→      PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled: true
   324→    SSESpecification:
   325→      SSEEnabled: true
   326→      SSEType: KMS
   327→      KMSMasterKeyId: !Ref FeeExemptionKMSKey
   328→    Tags:
   329→      - Key: Environment
   330→        Value: !Ref Environment
   331→      - Key: Application
   332→        Value: fee-exemption
   333→      - Key: DataClassification
   334→        Value: PII
   335→      - Key: Compliance
   336→        Value: LGPD
   337→```
   338→
   339→### 3. Lambda: Replay (REVISED)
   340→
   341→**Purpose**: Republish failed messages to the main SQS queue for reprocessing.
   342→
   343→**Configuration**:
   344→- Runtime: Node.js 20.x
   345→- Memory: 512 MB
   346→- Timeout: 5 minutes
   347→- **VPC**: Same VPC as FeeExemption Lambda (REQUIRED)
   348→- Triggers:
   349→  - API Gateway (manual replay)
   350→  - EventBridge Schedule (automatic daily replay)
   351→
   352→**CRITICAL: Atomic Status Transition**:
   353→```typescript
   354→// Prevent orphaned IN_PROGRESS records
   355→const updateToInProgress = async (cpfToken: string, createdAt: string, currentVersion: number) => {
   356→  const params: UpdateItemCommandInput = {
   357→    TableName: 'fee_exemption_failures',
   358→    Key: marshall({ cpf_token: cpfToken, created_at: createdAt }),
   359→    UpdateExpression: 'SET #status = :inProgress, #inProgressSince = :now, #version = :newVersion',
   360→    ConditionExpression: '#status = :pendingRetry AND #version = :currentVersion',
   361→    ExpressionAttributeNames: {
   362→      '#status': 'status',
   363→      '#inProgressSince': 'in_progress_since',
   364→      '#version': 'version'
   365→    },
   366→    ExpressionAttributeValues: marshall({
   367→      ':inProgress': 'IN_PROGRESS',
   368→      ':pendingRetry': 'PENDING_RETRY',
   369→      ':now': new Date().toISOString(),
   370→      ':currentVersion': currentVersion,
   371→      ':newVersion': currentVersion + 1
   372→    }),
   373→    ReturnValues: 'ALL_NEW'
   374→  };
   375→
   376→  return dynamodb.updateItem(params);
   377→};
   378→
   379→// Full replay flow with proper error handling
   380→const replaySingleRecord = async (record: FailureRecord): Promise<ReplayResult> => {
   381→  // 1. Atomically claim the record
   382→  try {
   383→    await updateToInProgress(record.cpf_token, record.created_at, record.version);
   384→  } catch (error) {
   385→    if (error.name === 'ConditionalCheckFailedException') {
   386→      return { status: 'SKIPPED', reason: 'Already being processed or status changed' };
   387→    }
   388→    throw error;
   389→  }
   390→
   391→  // 2. Publish to SQS
   392→  try {
   393→    await sqs.sendMessage({
   394→      QueueUrl: MAIN_QUEUE_URL,
   395→      MessageBody: JSON.stringify(record.payload),
   396→      MessageAttributes: {
   397→        idempotency_key: { DataType: 'String', StringValue: record.idempotency_key },
   398→        trace_id: { DataType: 'String', StringValue: record.trace_id },
   399→        replay_attempt: { DataType: 'Number', StringValue: String(record.retry_count + 1) }
   400→      }
   401→    });
   402→  } catch (error) {
   403→    // Rollback status on SQS failure
   404→    await updateStatusWithRetry(record.cpf_token, record.created_at, 'PENDING_RETRY');
   405→    throw error;
   406→  }
   407→
   408→  // 3. Update retry count
   409→  await incrementRetryCount(record.cpf_token, record.created_at);
   410→
   411→  return { status: 'REPLAYED' };
   412→};
   413→```
   414→
   415→**Circuit Breaker Integration**:
   416→```typescript
   417→interface CircuitBreakerState {
   418→  state: 'CLOSED' | 'OPEN' | 'HALF_OPEN';
   419→  failureCount: number;
   420→  lastFailureAt: string | null;
   421→  openUntil: string | null;
   422→}
   423→
   424→const CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PARAM = '/fee-exemption/circuit-breaker/state';
   425→const FAILURE_THRESHOLD = 5;
   426→const OPEN_DURATION_MS = 300000; // 5 minutes
   427→
   428→const checkCircuitBreaker = async (): Promise<boolean> => {
   429→  const state = await getCircuitBreakerState();
   430→
   431→  if (state.state === 'OPEN') {
   432→    if (new Date(state.openUntil!) > new Date()) {
   433→      logger.warn('Circuit breaker OPEN, skipping replay');
   434→      return false; // Skip replay
   435→    }
   436→    // Transition to HALF_OPEN
   437→    await updateCircuitBreakerState({ ...state, state: 'HALF_OPEN' });
   438→  }
   439→
   440→  return true;
   441→};
   442→
   443→const recordCircuitBreakerResult = async (success: boolean): Promise<void> => {
   444→  const state = await getCircuitBreakerState();
   445→
   446→  if (success) {
   447→    if (state.state === 'HALF_OPEN') {
   448→      await updateCircuitBreakerState({ state: 'CLOSED', failureCount: 0, lastFailureAt: null, openUntil: null });
   449→    }
   450→  } else {
   451→    const newFailureCount = state.failureCount + 1;
   452→    if (newFailureCount >= FAILURE_THRESHOLD) {
   453→      await updateCircuitBreakerState({
   454→        state: 'OPEN',
   455→        failureCount: newFailureCount,
   456→        lastFailureAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   457→        openUntil: new Date(Date.now() + OPEN_DURATION_MS).toISOString()
   458→      });
   459→      logger.error('Circuit breaker OPENED due to repeated failures');
   460→    } else {
   461→      await updateCircuitBreakerState({ ...state, failureCount: newFailureCount, lastFailureAt: new Date().toISOString() });
   462→    }
   463→  }
   464→};
   465→```
   466→
   467→**API Endpoints (with validation)**:
   468→
   469→```typescript
   470→// Request validation schemas
   471→const ReplayCpfRequestSchema = z.object({
   472→  cpfs: z.array(z.string().regex(/^\d{11}$/)).min(1).max(100)
   473→});
   474→
   475→const ReplayBatchRequestSchema = z.object({
   476→  status: z.enum(['PENDING_RETRY']),
   477→  limit: z.number().min(1).max(500).default(100),
   478→  older_than_hours: z.number().min(1).max(168).default(24)
   479→});
   480→
   481→// CPF validation with checksum
   482→const validateCpfChecksum = (cpf: string): boolean => {
   483→  const digits = cpf.split('').map(Number);
   484→
   485→  // First check digit
   486→  let sum = 0;
   487→  for (let i = 0; i < 9; i++) {
   488→    sum += digits[i] * (10 - i);
   489→  }
   490→  let check1 = 11 - (sum % 11);
   491→  if (check1 >= 10) check1 = 0;
   492→  if (check1 !== digits[9]) return false;
   493→
   494→  // Second check digit
   495→  sum = 0;
   496→  for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
   497→    sum += digits[i] * (11 - i);
   498→  }
   499→  let check2 = 11 - (sum % 11);
   500→  if (check2 >= 10) check2 = 0;
   501→  return check2 === digits[10];
   502→};
   503→```
   504→
   505→### 4. FeeExemption Lambda Enhancement (MANDATORY)
   506→
   507→**CRITICAL**: The existing FeeExemption Lambda MUST implement idempotency before replay system goes live.
   508→
   509→```typescript
   510→// Table: processed_exemptions
   511→// PK: idempotency_key
   512→
   513→const processExemption = async (message: FeeExemptionMessage): Promise<void> => {
   514→  const idempotencyKey = message.MessageAttributes?.idempotency_key?.StringValue
   515→    || generateIdempotencyKey(message);
   516→
   517→  // 1. Check if already processed
   518→  try {
   519→    await dynamodb.putItem({
   520→      TableName: 'processed_exemptions',
   521→      Item: marshall({
   522→        idempotency_key: idempotencyKey,
   523→        processed_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   524→        ttl: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) + (30 * 24 * 60 * 60) // 30 days
   525→      }),
   526→      ConditionExpression: 'attribute_not_exists(idempotency_key)'
   527→    });
   528→  } catch (error) {
   529→    if (error.name === 'ConditionalCheckFailedException') {
   530→      logger.info({ idempotencyKey }, 'Already processed, skipping');
   531→      return; // Idempotent success
   532→    }
   533→    throw error;
   534→  }
   535→
   536→  // 2. Call CBILL
   537→  try {
   538→    await callCBILL(message);
   539→  } catch (error) {
   540→    // Remove idempotency record on failure so it can be retried
   541→    await dynamodb.deleteItem({
   542→      TableName: 'processed_exemptions',
   543→      Key: marshall({ idempotency_key: idempotencyKey })
   544→    });
   545→    throw error;
   546→  }
   547→};
   548→```
   549→
   550→### 5. Stuck Record Recovery (NEW)
   551→
   552→**Purpose**: Detect and recover records stuck in IN_PROGRESS state.
   553→
   554→**EventBridge Rule**: Every 15 minutes
   555→```json
   556→{
   557→  "Name": "fee-exemption-stuck-recovery",
   558→  "ScheduleExpression": "rate(15 minutes)",
   559→  "State": "ENABLED",
   560→  "Target": {
   561→    "Arn": "arn:aws:lambda:...:fee-exemption-stuck-recovery",
   562→    "Input": "{\"max_stuck_minutes\": 30}"
   563→  }
   564→}
   565→```
   566→
   567→**Lambda: Stuck Recovery**:
   568→```typescript
   569→const handler = async (event: { max_stuck_minutes: number }) => {
   570→  const cutoff = new Date(Date.now() - event.max_stuck_minutes * 60 * 1000).toISOString();
   571→
   572→  // Query for stuck records across all shards
   573→  for (let shard = 0; shard < 10; shard++) {
   574→    const params: QueryCommandInput = {
   575→      TableName: 'fee_exemption_failures',
   576→      IndexName: 'status-created_at-index',
   577→      KeyConditionExpression: '#statusShard = :statusShard',
   578→      FilterExpression: '#inProgressSince < :cutoff',
   579→      ExpressionAttributeNames: {
   580→        '#statusShard': 'status_shard',
   581→        '#inProgressSince': 'in_progress_since'
   582→      },
   583→      ExpressionAttributeValues: marshall({
   584→        ':statusShard': `IN_PROGRESS#${shard}`,
   585→        ':cutoff': cutoff
   586→      })
   587→    };
   588→
   589→    const result = await dynamodb.query(params);
   590→    for (const item of result.Items || []) {
   591→      const record = unmarshall(item);
   592→      await resetToPendingRetry(record.cpf_token, record.created_at);
   593→      logger.warn({ cpfToken: record.cpf_token }, 'Reset stuck IN_PROGRESS record');
   594→    }
   595→  }
   596→};
   597→```

   917→
   918→## Operational Procedures
   919→
   920→### Required Runbooks
   921→
   922→1. **RB-001-High-Failure-Rate**: Response to >10 failures/hour
   923→2. **RB-002-Stale-Records**: Handling PENDING_RETRY records >7 days
   924→3. **RB-003-Manual-CPF-Replay**: Step-by-step API replay procedure
   925→4. **RB-004-CBILL-Outage**: Extended CBILL downtime response
   926→5. **RB-005-Circuit-Breaker-Open**: When auto-replay stops
   927→6. **RB-006-Stuck-InProgress**: Manual intervention for stuck records
   928→7. **RB-007-Rollback**: Full system rollback procedure
   929→
   930→### Rollback Procedure (DETAILED)
   931→
   932→```markdown
   933→## Full System Rollback
   934→
   935→### Pre-conditions
   936→- All team leads notified
   937→- Incident channel open
   938→
   939→### Steps
   940→
   941→1. **Pause auto-replay** (2 min)
   942→   ```bash
   943→   aws events disable-rule --name fee-exemption-auto-replay --region sa-east-1
   944→   aws events disable-rule --name fee-exemption-stuck-recovery --region sa-east-1
   945→   ```
   946→
   947→2. **Wait for IN_PROGRESS records** (up to 5 min)
   948→   ```bash
   949→   # Check for in-flight records
   950→   aws dynamodb scan \
   951→     --table-name fee_exemption_failures_prod \
   952→     --filter-expression "#s = :status" \
   953→     --expression-attribute-names '{"#s":"status"}' \
   954→     --expression-attribute-values '{":status":{"S":"IN_PROGRESS"}}' \
   955→     --select COUNT
   956→   ```
   957→   Wait until count = 0 or timeout after 5 minutes.
   958→
   959→3. **Remove DLQ event source mapping** (1 min)
   960→   ```bash
   961→   # Get mapping UUID
   962→   aws lambda list-event-source-mappings \
   963→     --function-name fee-exemption-dlq-consumer \
   964→     --query 'EventSourceMappings[0].UUID' --output text
   965→
   966→   # Delete mapping
   967→   aws lambda delete-event-source-mapping --uuid <UUID>
   968→   ```
   969→
   970→4. **Re-enable Step Function as DLQ consumer**
   971→   - Navigate to AWS Console > Step Functions
   972→   - Edit existing state machine to add SQS trigger
   973→   - OR deploy rollback CloudFormation template
   974→
   975→5. **Verify system state**
   976→   ```bash
   977→   # Confirm DLQ has no event source mappings
   978→   aws lambda list-event-source-mappings \
   979→     --event-source-arn arn:aws:sqs:sa-east-1:123456789012:SQS-Isencao_mensalidade_DLQ
   980→
   981→   # Should return empty
   982→   ```
   983→
   984→6. **Export pending records for manual processing**
   985→   ```bash
   986→   aws dynamodb scan \
   987→     --table-name fee_exemption_failures_prod \
   988→     --filter-expression "#s = :status" \
   989→     --expression-attribute-names '{"#s":"status"}' \
   990→     --expression-attribute-values '{":status":{"S":"PENDING_RETRY"}}' \
   991→     > pending_records.json
   992→   ```
   993→
   994→### Post-rollback
   995→- DynamoDB table retained (no data loss)
   996→- Records in PENDING_RETRY state need manual CBILL ticket
   997→- Schedule post-incident review
   998→```
   999→
  1000→---
  1001→
  1002→## Implementation Phases (REVISED)
  1003→
  1004→### Phase 0: Prerequisites (MANDATORY before Phase 1)
  1005→1. **FeeExemption Lambda idempotency implementation**
  1006→2. **Confirm CBILL behavior for duplicate requests**

  1008→4. **VPC endpoint for DynamoDB (if not exists)**
  1009→
  1010→### Phase 1: Core Infrastructure (MVP)
  1011→1. Create DynamoDB table with indexes
  1012→2. Deploy DLQ-Consumer Lambda
  1013→3. Configure Event Source Mapping with ReportBatchItemFailures
  1014→4. Deploy Replay Lambda with API Gateway
  1015→5. Create all CloudWatch alarms
  1016→
  1017→### Phase 2: Automation & Resilience
  1018→1. Configure EventBridge Schedule for auto-replay
  1019→2. Deploy stuck record recovery Lambda
  1020→3. Implement circuit breaker
  1021→4. Enhance Step Function email notifications
  1022→
  1023→### Phase 3: Observability & Operations
  1024→1. Create CloudWatch Dashboard
  1025→2. Enable X-Ray tracing
  1026→3. Write and publish all runbooks
  1027→4. Train squad Fenix on new workflow
  1028→5. Conduct game day exercise

  1031→
  1032→## Open Questions (RESOLVED)
  1033→
  1034→| Question | Resolution |
  1035→|----------|------------|
  1036→| CBILL Idempotency | MUST be handled in FeeExemption Lambda regardless of CBILL support |
  1037→| Ordering Requirements | Confirmed: promotions are period-specific, order doesn't matter |

  1048→|-----------|---------|-------------|
